title: Kritios Boy
description: Test your knowledge about this object.

assessment_items.question_content:
**The restrained naturalism of the *Kritios Boy* embodies which period of Greek art? **

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/086e3c8fd6c37de481b2cc5a140af2e0.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Sometimes described as Severe Style, the Kritios Boy demonstrates the transitional style that developed between the rigid forms of the Archaic period toward the fully integrated naturalism and dynamic figures of the Classical period in Greek art.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why do some scholars posit the Battle of Marathon important to the stylistic developments in sculptures like the *Kritios Boy*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Battle of Marathon in 490 was an important victory for the Greeks.  Some scholars believe the resulting sense of self propelled the shift toward greater naturalism in art.

choices.content: The Greek victory at Marathon led to an increased sense of self resulting in greater naturalism in art.
After Marathon, there was greater cultural exchange with Persian tradition to emphasize naturalism in art.
Athens emerged from Marathon as the most important of the Greek city-states resulting in sculptures of prominent Athenian athletes.
Following their defeat at Marathon, the Persian sack of the acropolis required the production of new sculptures for the sanctuary that looked different from earlier works.

assessment_items.question_content:
**The style and name of the *Kritios Boy* implies a relationship to the Greek technique of $\_\_\_\_\_\_$**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Named for its stylistic resemblance to bronze figures by sculptor Kritios, the *Kritios Boy* had inlaid eyes similar to  cast bronze sculptures.  

**[Watch this video to review.](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/ancient-greece/v/Kritios-boy)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**The contrapposto of the *Kritios Boy* can be seen in which details? **

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/086e3c8fd6c37de481b2cc5a140af2e0.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Contrapposto refers to the shifted weight of the figure, which impacts the alignment of the hips, knees, and shoulders.  

**[Watch this video to review.](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/ancient-greece/v/Kritios-boy)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Match the term that best applies to each object.**

[[☃ categorizer 1]]
hints: Characterized by their timeless expression and archaic smile,  Kouros figures exhibit symmetry and a frontal emphasis.

Characiterized by its naturalistic volumes and integrated anatomy, the *Kritios Boy* broke with the symmetry of archaic figures through the use of contrapposto and interest in complex relationships.  

**[Watch this video to review.](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/ancient-greece/v/Kritios-boy)**
